title: RNA method for nonlinear inversion
author: Kuang, Wenhuan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Considering the powerful ability of neural networks in representing high-dimensional functions, here we propose a reinforcement learning (RL) framework (namely RNA), which dynamically learns and constructs the global distribution of the objective function with a neural network, to realize moment tensor inversion. The learned objective function is then utilized to guide the adaptive resampling during each iteration, achieving both global optimization and enhanced inversion efficiency.
steps_raw: Considering the powerful ability of neural networks in representing high-dimensional functions, here we propose a reinforcement learning (RL) framework (namely RNA), which dynamically learns and constructs the global distribution of the objective function with a neural network, to realize moment tensor inversion. The learned objective function is then utilized to guide the adaptive resampling during each iteration, achieving both global optimization and enhanced inversion efficiency.
